name: Dads
description: Famous fathers, dads in TV and film, Father's Day, and the science of dad-hood.

questions.q: On what day is Father's Day celebrated in the United States?

source.notes: Made an official U.S. federal holiday by Richard Nixon in 1972.

questions.q: Who is generally credited with creating Father's Day in the U.S. in 1910?

source.notes: Dodd organized the first Father's Day celebration in Spokane, WA on June 19, 1910.

questions.q: Which family of male animals is the only one in nature where the male becomes pregnant?

source.original_a: They are the only male animals to become pregnant
source.notes: The female deposits eggs in the male's pouch; he carries them until they hatch.

questions.q: What hormone, sometimes called the 'love hormone', surges in dads (as well as moms) when bonding with infants?
What is the name of the syndrome in which expectant fathers experience pregnancy-like symptoms?
Which 'Simpsons' character is the bumbling, donut-loving dad of Bart, Lisa, and Maggie?
Which Atticus Finch novel is often cited as portraying the greatest fictional dad in American literature?

source.original_a: Atticus Finch from "To Kill a Mockingbird"
source.notes: Harper Lee's 1960 novel; played by Gregory Peck in the 1962 film.

questions.q: Which 1990 film features Macaulay Culkin alone at home while his parents are abroad?

source.notes: Dad-figure of the film: Daniel Stern's burglar.

questions.q: Who plays the dad in the 'Father of the Bride' (1991) film?

source.notes: Played George Banks; remake of the 1950 Spencer Tracy original.

questions.q: Which sitcom dad on 'Modern Family' is played by Ed O'Neill?

source.notes: O'Neill also played Al Bundy on 'Married… with Children'.

questions.q: Which classic '50s/'60s TV dad, played by Andy Griffith, was sheriff of Mayberry?

source.notes: Father of Opie (Ron Howard) on 'The Andy Griffith Show'.

questions.q: Which scientist, often called 'the father of evolution', was also a devoted dad to ten children?

source.notes: Famously open with his children, who became important readers and helpers.

questions.q: Which Roman 'father of the gods' shares his name with a planet?

source.notes: Equivalent to the Greek god Zeus.

questions.q: Which Moroccan emperor (1672–1727) reportedly fathered the largest verified number of children — at least 888?

source.notes: Also known as Ismail the Bloodthirsty. Listed in Guinness World Records.

questions.q: Which 18th-century Russian peasant's wife reportedly gave birth to 69 children?

source.notes: Including 16 sets of twins, 7 sets of triplets, and 4 sets of quadruplets.

questions.q: Which biology fact lets researchers trace deep paternal ancestry?
choices: Mitochondrial DNA passes only through fathers
The Y chromosome passes essentially unchanged from father to son
Bone density genes come from the father

source.original_a: The Y chromosome remains essentially unchanged as it is passed down through fathers
source.notes: Mitochondrial DNA actually passes through MOTHERS — common misconception.

questions.q: What does the male's chromosome contribute that determines the sex of human offspring?

source.original_a: Chromosomes from the father (X or Y)
source.notes: Sperm carry either X (→ female XX) or Y (→ male XY); the egg always contributes X.

questions.q: What classic Bill Cosby '80s sitcom centered on Cliff Huxtable, an obstetrician dad of five?
Which Bryan Cranston-led series features a dad of two who turns to making meth?

source.notes: Walter White also has a baby daughter (Holly) born late in the series.

questions.q: In Disney's 'Finding Nemo', what kind of fish is the protective dad Marlin?
